app.name: WikiBias Analyzer
app.description: Analyse the citation bias of Wikipedia articles: geographic origin, political pluralism and demographics of their sources.

header.tagline: Decoding the citation bias of Wikipedia articles

articleInput.ariaLabel: URL of the Wikipedia article to analyse
articleInput.errorInvalid: The URL must point to a Wikipedia article (e.g. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/...).

home.title: Are Wikipedia's sources biased?
home.lede: Enter an article URL to uncover bias in its cited sources across 5 criteria: geographic diversity, political pluralism, author parity, neutrality and reliability.

worldMap.title: Source map
worldMap.subtitle: Intensity by country
worldMap.ariaLabel: Choropleth map of the number of sources per country

sourcesTable.title: Sources
sourcesTable.rowToggleAria: Show the details of this source
sourcesTable.noDetails: No enriched data (Wikidata / MBFC / author) for this source.

note.generic_tld: Generic domain ({tld}): the TLD carries no country signal, and no publisher match was found in Wikidata or in the domain database.
note.no_country_signal: No country determined: neither the TLD, nor Wikidata, nor the domain database returned a match.
note.region_missing: Country “{country}” resolved, but missing from the region table (REGION_MAP) — the region stays unknown.

dashboard.unmappedNotice: {count} unmapped source ({pct}%) — country of origin not resolved via Wikidata or TLD. | {count} unmapped sources ({pct}%) — country of origin not resolved via Wikidata or TLD.

states.empty: No sources found. The article may have no standardised references.
states.error: This article could not be analysed. Check the URL and try again.
states.errorTimeout: The analysis timed out. Try again in a few minutes.
states.errorUnexpected: Unexpected state: {status}. Reload the page or check /status.
states.loadingSubtitle: Reading references, resolving Wikidata and computing distributions.
states.pendingSubtitle: A worker is processing this article. This page updates automatically.
states.pendingHint: Large articles can take several minutes.
states.pendingQueue: Queued: {n} analysis(es) ahead of this one.
states.pendingStalled: No progress for {quiet}. The analysis will be retried automatically.

footer.methodologyText: Geographic origin: curated domain database, then country-code TLD, then the publisher's country on Wikidata (P17/P495). Political leaning: Wikidata claims, then Media Bias/Fact Check. Author data from Wikidata is sourced; anything inferred from a name is an estimate and is marked as one. Unresolved sources are always shown with the reason, never hidden.
footer.projectText: Wikimania 2026 — Team 05E {team}. Open-source tool (MIT) deployed on Toolforge.

banner.title: v2 dashboard — experimental
banner.text: This view replaces the "bias profile" radar with the distributions the tool actually measures. Every number states where it came from, whether it is measured or estimated, and how much of the corpus it covers.

entry.title: v2 dashboard
entry.lede: The same analyses, read for traceability: where each number comes from, and how many sources it rests on.
entry.label: Wikipedia article URL
entry.invalid: That address is not a recognisable Wikipedia article.
entry.change1: The 5-axis radar is gone: three of its axes restated a distribution more crudely than the distribution itself does, and two read keys absent from the server's response, so they sat at zero for every article.
entry.change2: Every bar is clickable and opens the list of sources behind it.
entry.change3: Every attribute states its origin: Wikidata, MBFC, top-level domain, or an internal estimate.
entry.change4: Every metric shows its real coverage — "based on 6 of 8 sources" — never a figure implied to cover the whole corpus.

evidence.measuredLong: Measured — asserted by a checkable external source (Wikidata or MBFC)
evidence.estimatedLong: Estimated — inferred by this tool from a heuristic, with no external confirmation
evidence.absentLong: Unknown — no value could be determined. This is not a zero.

provenance.wikidataLong: A statement published on a Wikidata item
provenance.mbfcLong: A rating published by Media Bias/Fact Check
provenance.tldLong: Read off the top-level domain, with no confirmation from an identified publisher
provenance.heuristicLong: Estimated by this tool, with no external source
provenance.noneLong: No origin: the value was not determined

none.sources: None of the {total} sources supply this
none.authors: None of the {total} authors supply this

chart.excluded: {n} source(s) excluded from the chart ({pct}%): value not determined.

empty.missing: The server returned no data for this distribution. This is not a zero: the measurement never happened.
empty.allUnknown: No value could be determined for this corpus. Nothing is shown, rather than a chart of zeroes.

summary.wikidataHint: Sources whose publisher was matched to a Wikidata item.
summary.mbfcHint: Sources rated by Media Bias/Fact Check.
summary.geoMeasuredHint: Country corroborated by Wikidata or MBFC, not merely read off the domain.
summary.leanKnownHint: Sources for which an editorial leaning could be determined.

corpus.title: Where the sources come from
corpus.lede: Raw corpus distributions. Undetermined values are never drawn as a category: they leave the chart and are reported as a gap in coverage.

editorial.title: Editorial character
editorial.lede: Political leaning and reliability exactly as the server returns them, with no recomposition into an index.

authors.title: Who wrote these sources
authors.lede: The difference between a Wikidata statement and a guess from the spelling of a name is decisive here, and is never hidden.

text.title: Language signals
text.lede: Measurements over the text of the fetched sources, each with its real sample size.

geography.title: Country of publication
geography.subtitle: Number of sources per country
geography.detail: Confirmed by Wikidata or MBFC where available, otherwise read off the domain.

language.title: Language of sources
language.subtitle: Language detected in the fetched text
language.detail: Automatic language detection over the text.

sourceType.title: Source type
sourceType.subtitle: Editorial nature of the source
sourceType.detail: Classified internally from the domain and the citation.

leaning.title: Editorial leaning
leaning.subtitle: Raw values as returned by the server, not repositioned onto a left-right axis
leaning.detail: Ideology stated in Wikidata, or an MBFC bias rating.

reliability.title: Reliability
reliability.subtitle: Ordered scale: low, medium, high, academic
reliability.detail: An MBFC rating where one exists, otherwise an internal rule based on source type.

authorType.title: Author type
authorType.subtitle: A person or an organisation
authorType.detail: Inferred from the shape of the author's name.

authorNationality.title: Author nationality (estimated)
authorNationality.subtitle: Inferred from the linguistic origin of the name
authorNationality.detail: Heuristic estimate over first name and surname.

authorCitizenship.title: Author citizenship (Wikidata)
authorCitizenship.subtitle: Stated on the author's Wikidata item
authorCitizenship.detail: Covers only authors matched to a Wikidata item, so usually a small minority.

authorOccupation.title: Author occupations
authorOccupation.subtitle: Stated in Wikidata
authorOccupation.detail: Covers only authors present in Wikidata.

authorEmployer.title: Author employers
authorEmployer.subtitle: Stated in Wikidata
authorEmployer.detail: Rarely populated: the server most often returns an empty distribution.

authorSubregion.title: Author region (estimated)
authorSubregion.subtitle: Inferred from the linguistic origin of the name

languageBias.title: Subjectivity and sensationalism
languageBias.detail: Lexical scores computed by the server over the source text.

readability.title: Readability
readability.detail: Flesch reading-ease score, computed over the sources whose text could be fetched.

metrics.subjectivityHint: Scale of 0 to 1. Shown as measured: a subjectivity of 0 is not "100% neutral".
metrics.sensationalismHint: Scale of 0 to 1, from a lexicon of loaded terms.
metrics.opinionHint: Sources identified as op-eds or editorials.
metrics.fleschNote: The Flesch index is calibrated for English. On sources in another language it stays comparable between articles, but its absolute value means little.

gender.title: Author gender: measured and estimated
gender.lede: Two separate channels, never added together. Wikidata publishes a statement about a named person; the heuristic guesses from the spelling of a first name and surname. Merging them would produce a number that describes neither.
gender.noAuthors: No author profiles were extracted from these sources.
gender.measuredDetail: A gender statement carried by the author's Wikidata item.
gender.estimatedDetail: Heuristic estimate from the linguistic origin of the first name and surname.

none.measured: None of the {total} authors has a gender stated in Wikidata. This is not "0% women": it is the absence of any confirmed data.
none.estimated: The heuristic could not assign a gender to any of the {total} authors.

gender.undetermined: {n} of {total} author(s) have no gender determined by either channel.
gender.disagreeTitle: {n} disagreement(s) between Wikidata and the estimate
gender.disagreeNote: These cases alone justify keeping the two channels apart: the aggregate the server publishes is built on the estimate and therefore inherits these errors.

disagree.missed: the heuristic concluded nothing (confidence {confidence}%), Wikidata states: {measured}

gender.aggNote: The two distributions the server computes itself. v1 read the key author_gender_distribution, which does not exist, and therefore always got zero. They are reproduced here for checking, but are not used as the headline figure because they rest on the estimate.

derived.title: Derived indicators
derived.lede: The only figures on this page not returned directly by the server. Each one shows its formula and its exact inputs, so the arithmetic can be redone by hand.

reading.geography: {pct}% of located sources come from a single country: {top}.
reading.language: {pct}% of identified sources are in a single language: {top}.

derived.caveat: Computed over known buckets only, so a corpus that is largely unidentified cannot read as a diverse one.
derived.empty: No derived indicator can be computed: the underlying distributions are empty.

ledger.title: Source ledger
ledger.lede: The full record that every chart above is a count over. Each attribute carries its origin and, where one exists, a link to the corresponding statement.
